mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and subsequently positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the labor force participation rate signals a strengthening labor market, leading to increased consumer spending and subsequently negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the goods producers' inventory level signals a weakening supply chain, leading to reduced production and subsequently positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the consumer price index signals a weakening demand for goods and services, leading to reduced spending and subsequently positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.06).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate signals a strengthening labor market, leading to increased consumer spending and subsequently negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the goods producers' inventory level signals a weakening supply chain, leading to reduced production and subsequently positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.04).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.03).
